prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.562) and high seasonality variance (1.0358), with early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, ONLY_DWT_rf) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf, ONLY_CWT_catboost). FT_catbest leads trend tracking while DWT_catboost dominates overall RMSE. The combination of high disagreement and seasonal variance strongly supports DBA combination to leverage diverse model strengths across horizons.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_rf", "late": "ONLY_CWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "DWT_svr", "DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "FT_svr", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_rf", "rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "FT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.562,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.562) and high seasonality variance (1.0358), with early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, ONLY_DWT_rf) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf, ONLY_CWT_catboost). FT_catbest leads trend tracking while DWT_catboost dominates overall RMSE. The combination of high disagreement and seasonal variance strongly supports DBA combination to leverage diverse model strengths across horizons."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. RMSE spread_ratio=0.562 and high_seasonality_variance=1.0358 from pattern_analyst strongly support DBA combination as recommended_method_hint. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate to avoid instability. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 adds shrinkage for robustness. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, ONLY_DWT_rf) while dba_combination handles phase-shifted peaks across horizons."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, selection candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding from past windows only, weighted candidate uses validation_summary.n_windows=3 with expanding mode. Score margin top2 is small (0.010), indicating competitive set. Universe leaderboards show current candidates are among top performers (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 leads POCID at 75.76). No redundancy requiring pruning - all 4 candidates serve different purposes.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.562) and high seasonality variance (1.0358), with early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, ONLY_DWT_rf) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf, ONLY_CWT_catboost). FT_catbest leads trend tracking while DWT_catboost dominates overall RMSE. The combination of high disagreement and seasonal variance strongly supports DBA combination to leverage diverse model strengths across horizons.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. RMSE spread_ratio=0.562 and high_seasonality_variance=1.0358 from pattern_analyst strongly support DBA combination as recommended_method_hint. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate to avoid instability. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 adds shrinkage for robustness. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, ONLY_DWT_rf) while dba_combination handles phase-shifted peaks across horizons.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, selection candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding from past windows only, weighted candidate uses validation_summary.n_windows=3 with expanding mode. Score margin top2 is small (0.010), indicating competitive set. Universe leaderboards show current candidates are among top performers (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 leads POCID at 75.76). No redundancy requiring pruning - all 4 candidates serve different purposes.
